name: Cessna 182
description: Small single used for hop and pops

name: Sabre2 190
description: First sport main after student gear
Level 3 — stable freefall the whole way
Waved at the camera mid-freefall like I was on a cruise ship. Forgot to fall. Instructor docked while crying with laughter and later said I looked like a golden retriever who just discovered gravity.

Textbook landing into a standing ovation from my own ego — then high-fived a packing mat at full strut and faceplanted into the peas like a lawn dart. The packing area gave me a slow clap. I deserved it.
Sidebody exit went rogue. Gripped a stranger's boot instead of their hand and rode them like a confused koala for the entire freefall. They still don't make eye contact in the packing hangar.
Altimeter yeeted itself into the void at exit. Spent the whole freefall screaming "WHAT TIME IS IT" at the formation like a deranged town crier. Nobody answered. Pull altitude was a vibe check.

Halloween load. Genius in a cape exited and instantly became a 120 mph nylon banshee thrashing through the formation. We scattered like pigeons hit by a leaf blower. Cape later found wrapped around a windsock. Legend status: cursed.

Last load of the season. Landed directly into the beer line like a precision strike. Entire packing tent erupted into a full-volume chant of my name for reasons science cannot explain. I bowed. Someone handed me a hot dog. Peak civilization.

Committed to head-down with the confidence of a man who has never known fear — immediately cartwheeled into a sit like a washing machine on spin cycle. Coach high-fived me mid-air like it was intentional. I nodded solemnly. We both knew.
Long track to the open field

Locked onto a Mediterranean seagull like it owed me money. Full-commitment track. Bird banked left with contempt. I overshot into empty sky. Seagull is still telling this story in the bars of Empuriabrava.
Long track to the open field

Nailed every grip. Hit every point. Felt unstoppable. Ground debrief: I had been facing completely backwards the entire freefall. The formation had just built around my confused rear end. Video evidence exists. I have seen it. I can never unsee it.

100th jump cake load. Attempted a celebratory canopy high-five at 200 feet. Physics declined. Both of us nearly collapsed into the peas like poorly stacked lawn furniture. Survived. Cake happened. Frosting was in my ears. Worth every second.

Four-way randoms went great until it didn't. Pulled at 1000 m and the main came out looking like a cinnamon roll with opinions. Spun right like it had somewhere important to be. Kicked twice. The canopy kicked back harder. Cut away around 700 m while inventing several new words. Reserve opened like a saint. Then I flared into the only cow pie in a three-county radius. Soft landing. Fragrance: bold. Gear check later found a misrouted rear riser. No injuries. Dignity not recovered. Main found in a tree looking embarrassed.

Flew like a majestic eagle for approximately four seconds then entered what experts are calling a "dramatic tumbling burrito phase." Screamed the entire way. A crop duster pilot later asked if I was okay. I was not. Still the best jump ever.
